raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 535 establishes clear guidelines for how companies from the olive oil industry can use the term 'California' in their labeling and marketing.",
    "The measure will protect consumers and farmers by providing clear information about the source of the olives and olive oils in the products they buy.",
    "California has the best agricultural products and the highest environmental and labor standards in the world.",
    "Consumers associate California-grown foods with quality.",
    "The demand for California olive oil is steadily increasing due to its established reputation for producing high-quality olive oils.",
    "Blended olive oils branded as 'California' may contain as little as 14 percent of Californian olive oil.",
    "AB 535 provides consumers with clear information about what they are purchasing regarding olive oil.",
    "AB 535 does not restrict blending oils from different sources but ensures truthful disclosure when 'California' is used.",
    "The California Olive Oil Commission has found several brands of California olive oil that mislead the public by using ambiguous labeling.",
    "The California olive oil industry produces approximately 4% of the world’s olive oil from over 75 varieties of olives."
  ]
}
```
